title: Healthcare Freedom and Choice Act
plain_summary: This bill cancels a new government rule about short-term health insurance plans that was made in April 2024. The rule said these plans could only last for 3 months at first and no more than 4 months total, even with renewals. Before this rule, short-term plans could last up to 12 months at first and up to 36 months total with renewals. By canceling the rule, this bill would let short-term health plans go back to lasting longer like they used to.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on Education and Workforce, and Ways and Means, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each c
subjects: Department of Health and Human Services

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Healthcare Freedom and Choice Act.

sections.title: Section 2  Short-Term, Limited Duration Insurance Rule Nullification
sections.text: A rule about short-term health insurance was published on April third, two thousand twenty-four by three government agencies. These agencies were the Internal Revenue Service, the Employee Benefits Security Administration, and the Health and Human Services Department. The rule was about short-term health insurance plans that last for a limited time and other health benefits that work independently. This rule will have no power and cannot be enforced.
